verdict: PASS — all 7 checks (plus format discipline and diagram-embed) passed with no findings. Reading is faithful to corpus, well-scoped, beginner-readable, and correctly formatted.

format_discipline.notes: First non-empty line is the H1 title; no YAML frontmatter. Sidecar parses cleanly with all required fields. Six required ## headings present in correct order.

length.notes: 1432 / 230 = 6.23 min. Both word count and reading time within target.

citations.key_claims_with_citation_coverage: high — all substantive assertions carry citation markers; estimated >85% coverage
citations.notes: Resource [1] cited in every major section. [2] and [3] used consistently for supporting claims. No orphaned markers or bibliography entries.

prereq_alignment.notes: All terms used are either in concepts_introduced for this topic or the prior-vocabulary list. References to 'topic 1.2 determinism test' are valid callbacks to prior vocabulary. Language models named as examples without teaching internals.

sample_summary.assertion: A probabilistic system is one where the output is not fully fixed by the input.
Probability measured on a scale from 0 to 1.
Four-row comparison table (AI story generator, weather, spam filter, dice).
Randomness is a deliberate design tool, not chaos.
Sampling — picking one answer from a range of possible answers.
Temperature setting controls variation when sampling.
Do not assume variation means error — check whether system is designed probabilistic.
Three-step determinism test applied to AI writing assistant.

faithfulness.notes: All 10 sampled assertions map verbatim or paraphrased to corpus. No novel claims introduced.

beginner_readability.notes: All non-everyday terms (probabilistic system, probability, uncertainty, randomness, sampling, output variation, temperature) are bolded and defined on first use in separate subsections. Each concept is introduced with a concrete example or analogy before the abstraction. Sentence lengths are short and plain-register throughout. Zero hedging phrases detected.

formatting.notes: All paragraphs are 3–5 sentences. Multi-item enumerations (randomness examples, best-practice habits, takeaways) are rendered as bullet or numbered lists. Inline examples appear in Key Concepts beyond the Worked Example section. Defined terms consistently use bold-term pattern.

assessment_reference.notes: No assessment IDs, due-week references, or grade-weight language found anywhere in the reading.

scope.notes: Temperature is in concepts_introduced and is covered appropriately with a disclaimer that internals are not required. Spam filters, weather forecasting, and medical diagnosis are used as named examples without teaching their mechanisms. No ML classifiers, training data, statistical models, or LLM internals taught.

diagram_embedded.notes: diagram.png referenced inline at line 59 with a descriptive alt-text and a caption on line 60 explaining the deterministic vs probabilistic flow contrast.
